meeting_notes: Renewal discussion - confirm renewal date discrepancy before meeting
email_summary: Strauss placed Q1 and Q2 orders with Cana Doce Açúcar for 60 MT of ICUMSA 45 refined white sugar at $495/MT each, both of which were fulfilled on time and confirmed received in good order. The supplier has proactively opened renewal pricing discussions, proposing $510/MT for the renewal period and sharing a tiered volume discount structure. Two unresolved issues remain: a discrepancy between the parties' records on the contract renewal date (Strauss: September 30, 2027; supplier: October 15, 2027), and the supplier's $510/MT renewal proposal, which has not been formally accepted.

contract_terms.payment_terms: Net 30 from bill of lading date
contract_terms.key_penalty: Late delivery beyond agreed window: 1% of shipment value per week delayed, capped at 8%.

open_issues.description: Strauss's signed contract (STR-SUGAR-2025-018) shows a renewal date of September 30, 2027, but Fernando Almeida referenced October 15, 2027 in his March 15, 2026 email and this was not resolved in the subsequent March 28 reply. As of the June 10 email, the supplier still has not confirmed the correct date despite saying they would revert.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-15 from Fernando Almeida; email 2026-03-28 from Fernando Almeida; contract STR-SUGAR-2025-018 Renewal Date clause

open_issues.description: Supplier has formally proposed a renewal price of $510/MT for the post-renewal period in the June 10, 2026 email, representing a $15/MT increase over the current contract price of $495/MT. Strauss has acknowledged receipt but not accepted or countered; a call is scheduled for June 23, 2026.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-10 from Fernando Almeida; email 2026-06-15 from David Cohen

commodity_benchmark: ICE No.11 sugar futures have declined from approximately 15.0 ¢/lb on March 30, 2026 to 13.59 ¢/lb on June 15, 2026 — a fall of roughly 9.4% over the past 11 weeks — which weakens the supplier's justification for a $510/MT price increase and gives Strauss market-trend leverage in the upcoming renewal negotiation.
heads_up: The June 23 renewal call is imminent and the supplier is proposing $510/MT (+$15/MT, ~+3%) for renewal, but the ICE No.11 benchmark has dropped ~9% since late March, directly undercutting the supplier's cost-pressure argument. Resolve the renewal date discrepancy (September 30 vs. October 15, 2027) before entering formal negotiations, as the correct date determines when the 90-day non-renewal notice window opens.
negotiation_brief: Cana Doce Açúcar is a reliable, well-performing supplier — both Q1 and Q2 shipments were delivered on time and at spec with no disputes, and the relationship is professionally managed. The supplier is requesting $510/MT for the renewal, a $15/MT (3%) increase, citing ICE No.11 trends and Santos logistics costs; however, the benchmark data shows ICE No.11 has fallen from 15.0 ¢/lb to 13.59 ¢/lb over the past 11 weeks, which squarely contradicts the cost-pressure narrative and gives Strauss a strong basis to push back or hold at $495/MT. Walking into the June 23 call, the single most important priority is to pin down the correct contract renewal date — the supplier has not yet produced their executed copy, and the discrepancy between September 30 and October 15, 2027 must be resolved before Strauss commits to any negotiating timeline, since a two-week error affects when the 90-day non-renewal notice deadline falls.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Good — all shipments on time: Q1 departed February 2 as scheduled, arrived February 22 (confirmed February 24); Q2 departed May 5 as scheduled, arrived May 26 (confirmed May 27/28).

benchmark.source: ICE Sugar No.11 Futures (SB=F) via Yahoo Finance

raw_emails.subject: Greetings and Q1 Order Confirmation
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

I trust this message finds you well and that you had a restful holiday period. On behalf of Cana Doce Açúcar, I wish you and your team a prosperous 2026.

I am writing to confirm our readiness to fulfill the Q1 order per our current contract terms. Please send the official purchase order at your earliest convenience so that we may initiate the production scheduling process.

As a matter of standard practice, I wish to note: all Cana Doce Açúcar pricing is quoted in USD. Pursuant to our contract terms, the applicable exchange rate is that of the invoice issue date. Any currency conversion costs are borne by the buyer.

Kind regards,
Fernando Almeida
Sales Director, Cana Doce Açúcar

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 Order Confirmation
raw_emails.body:
Fernando,

Thank you and likewise — hope 2026 is a good year for both companies.

Q1 PO is attached: 60 tons ICUMSA 45, per contract pricing of $495/ton, delivery by February 28. Please confirm.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 Order Confirmation — PO Acknowledged
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for the purchase order. I can confirm receipt and acceptance of PO-2026-0041 for 60 metric tons of ICUMSA 45 refined white sugar at USD 495/MT, with delivery requested by February 28, 2026.

I have copied Isabela Souza from our logistics team, who will coordinate the export documentation and shipping schedule directly with you.

Best regards,
Fernando Almeida

raw_emails.subject: Shipment logistics — PO-2026-0041
raw_emails.body:
Dear Mr. Cohen,

Good afternoon. I am Isabela Souza, responsible for export logistics at Cana Doce Açúcar.

The 60-ton shipment is scheduled to depart Santos port on February 2, 2026 aboard the MV Belém Star. Estimated arrival at Ashdod is February 22 — well within your requested delivery window and consistent with our standard 21-day CIF transit time to Ashdod.

Please find the draft bill of lading attached for your review.

With kind regards,
Isabela Souza
Logistics Coordinator, Cana Doce Açúcar

raw_emails.subject: Vessel departed Santos
raw_emails.body:
Dear Mr. Cohen,

Please be advised that the MV Belém Star departed Santos port on February 2, 2026 as scheduled. Tracking reference: BL-CNS-20260202-447. Current ETA at Ashdod: February 22, 2026 — same lead time as committed.

With kind regards,
Isabela Souza

raw_emails.subject: Q1 shipment received
raw_emails.body:
Isabela,

Confirming receipt of the Q1 shipment — 60 tons arrived in good order, quality inspection passed, ICUMSA 45 confirmed. Invoice processing underway.

Thank you for the smooth coordination.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 Planning and Commercial Discussion
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

I hope you are well. I am reaching out proactively to begin Q2 planning. Based on our current contract I anticipate a similar volume of 55-65 tons for Q2. Please confirm your intended volume when convenient.

Additionally, I wish to raise a forward-looking commercial topic. As we approach our renewal date of October 15, 2027, our pricing team has been reviewing contract terms. We believe a modest adjustment for the renewal period will be warranted given input cost trends. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss this on a call at your convenience.

I also wanted to share, for your planning purposes, our tiered pricing structure for the renewal period:
- 50-74 MT/quarter: base contract rate
- 75-99 MT/quarter: 2.0% discount below base rate
- 100 MT/quarter and above: 4.5% discount below base rate

Your current run-rate of approximately 60 MT/quarter sits just below the first discount tier. An increase to 75 MT would qualify for the 2.0% discount — at the current base price of $495/MT that would be a saving of approximately $5,940 per quarter. Worth considering as you plan ahead.

Best regards,
Fernando Almeida

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 Planning and Commercial Discussion
raw_emails.body:
Fernando,

Thanks for the proactive reach-out. For Q2 we are planning 60 tons again — same as Q1. Isabela can start the scheduling.

On the renewal discussion — happy to have that call closer to the date. Let's revisit in July or August.

On the volume tiers — interesting, I will share with my manager. A move to 75 MT/quarter is not something I can commit to right now but I'll keep it in mind for planning.

One thing to note on the renewal date: our records show the current contract runs to September 30, 2027 (24-month term from October 2025). You mentioned October 15 — can you confirm which date your team is working from?

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 shipment schedule
raw_emails.body:
Dear Mr. Cohen,

Thank you for confirming Q2 volume of 60 metric tons. Proposed schedule:
- Departure from Santos: May 5, 2026
- Estimated arrival at Ashdod: May 26, 2026

This is within our standard 21-day CIF window. Please send the Q2 purchase order at your earliest convenience.

With kind regards,
Isabela Souza

raw_emails.subject: RE: Renewal date — clarification
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for flagging the renewal date discrepancy. I will check with our contracts team and revert. It is possible there was an administrative error on our side when the renewed contract was issued. I want to make sure we are both working from the same document.

Best regards,
Fernando Almeida

raw_emails.subject: Q2 PO — Cana Doce
raw_emails.body:
Isabela,

Q2 PO attached: 60 tons ICUMSA 45 at $495/ton, delivery by May 30. Please confirm.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 PO — confirmed
raw_emails.body:
Dear Mr. Cohen,

PO-2026-0088 confirmed and accepted. We will proceed with loading arrangements for the May 5 departure. Documentation will follow.

With kind regards,
Isabela Souza

raw_emails.subject: Q2 vessel departed Santos
raw_emails.body:
Dear Mr. Cohen,

The Q2 shipment of 60 metric tons departed Santos port on May 5 as scheduled, aboard the MV Primavera Sul. Tracking reference: BL-CNS-20260505-619. Estimated arrival at Ashdod: May 26, 2026 — within our quoted 21-day CIF window, consistent with Q1.

With kind regards,
Isabela Souza

raw_emails.subject: Q2 shipment received
raw_emails.body:
Isabela,

Q2 arrived May 27 — 60 tons, quality confirmed ICUMSA 45. All good. Invoice will be processed this week.

David

raw_emails.subject: Renewal preparation + renewal date
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

I trust you are well. I am writing to formally initiate the commercial discussion for the renewal period.

Our pricing team has completed its review. For the renewal we are proposing an adjustment to $510/MT, reflecting the ICE No.11 trend over the past 12 months and increased logistics costs from Santos.

Regarding the renewal date — I checked with our contracts team. Their records show October 15, 2027. I understand your records show September 30, 2027. I am requesting a copy of the executed contract from our legal department to resolve this. I will revert once I have that confirmation.

I would suggest a call in late June to discuss pricing. Would the week of June 22 suit you?

Best regards,
Fernando Almeida

raw_emails.subject: RE: Renewal preparation
raw_emails.body:
Fernando,

Thanks for the advance notice. Week of June 22 works — let's say June 23 at 14:00 Israel / 09:00 São Paulo.

On the $510/MT proposal — I'll review with my team before the call.

On the renewal date — please do send me a copy of the executed contract once you have it from your legal team. I want to make sure we are both referencing the same document before we enter formal renewal negotiations. Our signed copy clearly shows September 30, 2027.

David

raw_contracts.supplier_05_cana_doce_OLD.md:
# Contract Summary — Cana Doce Açúcar (EXPIRED — superseded)

**Contract ID:** STR-SUGAR-2024-011
**Supplier:** Cana Doce Açúcar (Brazil)
**Category:** Sugar — refined white sugar (ICUMSA 45)
**Effective Date:** 2024-10-01
**Term:** 12 months
**Expiry Date:** 2025-09-30 — **CONTRACT EXPIRED. Superseded by STR-SUGAR-2025-018.**

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from bill of lading date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 50 tons/quarter, maximum 80 tons/quarter.

## Pricing
$485/ton, fixed for the 12-month contract term. No benchmark adjustment mechanism — price was locked at signing.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond agreed window: 1% of shipment value per week delayed, capped at 8%.
- Quality failure (ICUMSA reading exceeds 45 on independent test): full replacement shipment at supplier cost, no charge to Strauss.

## Notes
This contract expired September 30, 2025 and was renewed under STR-SUGAR-2025-018. Retained for historical reference only. Key difference vs. the renewal: pricing was fixed at $485/ton with no benchmark linkage; the renewed contract introduced a benchmark review mechanism after 12 months. Formal, reliable supplier — no significant disputes on record under this contract.
raw_contracts.supplier_05_cana_doce_RENEWED.md:
# Contract Summary — Cana Doce Açúcar (ACTIVE — renewed)

**Contract ID:** STR-SUGAR-2025-018
**Supplier:** Cana Doce Açúcar (Brazil)
**Category:** Sugar — refined white sugar (ICUMSA 45)
**Effective Date:** 2025-10-01
**Term:** 24 months
**Renewal Date:** 2027-09-30 (auto-renews for 12 months unless either party provides 90 days written notice of non-renewal)

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from bill of lading date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 55 tons/quarter, maximum 85 tons/quarter (increased from the prior contract).

## Pricing
$495/ton, fixed for the first 12 months of the contract term (through September 30, 2026). After 12 months, pricing subject to benchmark review against ICE No.11 futures with 30 days written notice required from either party for any proposed adjustment. Agreed adjustments require written confirmation from both parties before taking effect.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond agreed window: 1% of shipment value per week delayed, capped at 8%.
- Quality failure (ICUMSA reading exceeds 45 on independent test): full replacement shipment at supplier cost, no charge to Strauss.

## Notes
Currently active contract. Cross-reference against email thread: in a March 2026 email, the supplier contact Fernando Almeida refers to "our October 15th renewal" — this is approximately two weeks off from the actual contract renewal date of 2027-09-30. The email appears to conflate "renewal" with the end of the fixed-price period (September 30, 2026, when the benchmark review kicks in), but the wording is ambiguous and should be flagged. The prototype should surface this date discrepancy and prompt the Procurement Manager to verify what the supplier believes the renewal date is before the next negotiation meeting.
